title: Grove School of Engineering

infobox.value: (**GSoE**) is the [engineering school](/wiki/Engineering_education "Engineering education") of the [City College of New York](/wiki/City_College_of_New_York "City College of New York") (CCNY), a [public university](/wiki/Public_university "Public university") system in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and part of the [City University of New York](/wiki/City_University_of_New_York "City University of New York") (CUNY) system. It is one of the five schools of City College and CUNY's primary school of [engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering").
["The future is engineered here"](https://www.ccny.cuny.edu/engineering). *Website*. CCNY. Retrieved September 29, 2016.

["Intel's Grove Gives $26 Million to CCNY'S School of Engineering"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161002120129/http://www1.cuny.edu/mu/forum/2005/10/28/intels-grove-gives-26-million-to-ccnys-school-of-engineering/). CCNY. October 28, 2005. Archived from [the original](http://www1.cuny.edu/mu/forum/2005/10/28/intels-grove-gives-26-million-to-ccnys-school-of-engineering/) on October 2, 2016. Retrieved September 29, 2016.

Arenson, Karen W. (2005-10-27). ["City College Gets $26 Million From Former Intel Chairman"](https://www.nytimes.com/2005/10/27/education/city-college-gets-26-million-from-former-intel-chairman.html). *The New York Times*. Retrieved 2023-11-01.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [40°49′10″N 73°57′00″W﻿ / ﻿40.8194°N 73.9500°W﻿ / 40.8194; -73.9500](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Grove_School_of_Engineering&params=40.8194_N_73.95_W_region:US_type:edu)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

City University of New York's school of engineering

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/55/City_College_of_New_St._Paul%27s_Chapel_%28Columbia_University%29_-_Grove_School_of_Engineering_%2848170351541%29.jpg/250px-City_College_of_New_St._Paul%27s_Chapel_%28Columbia_University%29_-_Grove_School_of_Engineering_%2848170351541%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:City_College_of_New_St._Paul%27s_Chapel_(Columbia_University)_-_Grove_School_of_Engineering_(48170351541).jpg)

The Grove School of Engineering—Steinman Hall at City College of New York

The **Grove School of Engineering** (**GSoE**) is the [engineering school](/wiki/Engineering_education "Engineering education") of the [City College of New York](/wiki/City_College_of_New_York "City College of New York") (CCNY), a [public university](/wiki/Public_university "Public university") system in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and part of the [City University of New York](/wiki/City_University_of_New_York "City University of New York") (CUNY) system. It is one of the five schools of City College and CUNY's primary school of [engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering").

The GSoE is housed in the [Steinman Hall](/w/index.php?title=Steinman_Hall&action=edit&redlink=1 "Steinman Hall (page does not exist)"), located in the northern side of the City College of New York's campus. It offers undergraduate and graduate [engineering education](/wiki/Engineering_education "Engineering education"). It includes 15 [research institutes](/wiki/Research_institute "Research institute") covering all major areas of engineering, including: Biomedical engineering, chemical engineering, electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, civil engineering, and environmental engineering.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The school currently has over five hundred students and 110 faculty on staff.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In October 2005, the former CCNY Engineering School became the Grove School of Engineering after [Andrew Grove](/wiki/Andrew_Grove "Andrew Grove"), an alumnus of the school and co-founder of the [Intel Corporation](/wiki/Intel_Corporation "Intel Corporation"), made the largest single donation that the CCNY had ever received. Grove's donation of $26 million was used to fund research and equipment.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
